name: Ko Olina Lagoon 3
tagline: Year-round protected snorkeling with turtles — the safest, most accessible snorkel on Oahu.
parking: Free public lot for Lagoon 3 (fills by 9am weekends)
bestTime: Arrive by 8am weekends for parking; weekdays are easy. Morning has best visibility
talkStory: Ko Olina means 'place of joy' and these lagoons were carved from the reef in the 1990s, with breakwaters designed to create perpetually calm water. Lagoon 3 draws the most honu because its deeper channel grows the limu (seaweed) turtles feed on. Stay 10+ feet from turtles — NOAA monitors this beach and violations carry federal fines up to $21,000. Weekday mornings you can have the lagoon to yourself before resort guests wake up.
